summary.intent: Implement the NavigationMenu framework-agnostic core for ars-ui issue #264, scoped strictly to ars-components with no framework adapter work, DOM handles, positioning, or z-index allocation.
summary.outcome: Successfully implemented the full NavigationMenu core (state machine, events, effects, context, props, messages, SubProps, parts, API, attrs) with 45 tests and 13 insta snapshots; all verification gates passed and a non-draft PR was opened with Closes #264.

repo: Component tests and snapshots live inline under crates/ars-components/src/navigation/<component>/ rather than in the older external tests/spec_conformance/navigation/ directories.
`cargo xtask spec info <component-name>` resolves both the component spec and its shared dependencies (e.g. z-index-stacking.md).
`cargo xci-fast` is the required fast CI gate before any commit/push; it runs fmt, check, clippy, and nextest across the workspace.
Post-implementation audit via .agents/skills/post-implementation-audit/SKILL.md is mandatory and all findings must land in the same PR.

The Leptos and Dioxus adapter specs reference `navigation_menu::ItemDef` but neither the core NavigationMenu spec nor the core implementation defines that type — known adapter-spec drift left unresolved.

The repo already has many pre-existing unreferenced insta snapshots in unrelated components, causing `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` to fail repo-wide; NavigationMenu snapshots must be verified with a filtered run instead.
Unrelated failing tests in the `progress` and `meter` components prevent the unfiltered `cargo mutants` baseline from passing, requiring a focused `-- --lib navigation_menu` filter for mutation runs.

code.finding: New file: full NavigationMenu agnostic core — State, Event, Effect, Context, Props, SubProps, Messages, Part, Api; typed Effect enum (not stringly typed); no DOM/timer/z-index APIs.
Extended with NavigationMenu spec-conformance coverage asserting Part enum data-ars-scope/data-ars-part output, required ARIA surface, and absence of z-index public surface.

workflow: Mutation testing should be run with `-- --lib <module>` to isolate from unrelated failing tests in the workspace baseline.
Use `cargo mutants -F <line:col>` with `--minimum-test-timeout 60` to recheck specific surviving/timeout mutants without rerunning the full set.
First mutation pass typically exposes real test gaps (43 missed initially); batch-fix all misses before a second full run rather than patching one at a time.
The `--unreferenced=reject` flag for insta is not safe to use workspace-wide if unrelated components have accumulated unreferenced snapshots; prefer per-component filtered runs.
SubProps for nested/sub-navigation instances was in the spec but missing from the initial implementation — always check the spec for nested component configuration types during audit.

friction: `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` fails globally due to pre-existing unreferenced snapshots in unrelated components, making it impossible to use the intended verification command.
Unrelated `progress`/`meter` value-text test failures break the unfiltered `cargo mutants` baseline, requiring a workaround focused filter for every mutation run.
First mutation run found 43 missed mutants, requiring a significant second round of test writing before the audit could be closed.
Mutation runs are slow (isolated scratch-tree compilations per candidate), and the polling loop required many `write_stdin` ticks while waiting.
The `waiting-for-codex-review` delivery workflow involves a large number of polling operations with no native async notification.
open_items: Leptos and Dioxus adapter specs reference `navigation_menu::ItemDef` which is undefined in the core spec and unimplemented in core — needs resolution (define in core contract or remove from adapter sketches).
NavigationMenu Leptos and Dioxus adapter implementations are explicitly out of scope for this task and still need to be done.
Z-index allocation for NavigationMenu submenus is adapter-owned; no core validation exists beyond ensuring stable attrs are exposed — adapter implementations will need to wire this up.
Pre-existing unrelated unreferenced insta snapshots across the repo should be cleaned up to make `--unreferenced=reject` usable globally.
Pre-existing `progress`/`meter` value-text test failures in the repo are unrelated to this task but block the default mutation baseline and should be fixed.
